title: Gobi Matar Sabzi with Roti
description: Cauliflower and peas in a classic onion-tomato masala, served with rotis as a complete vegetable main.
explanation: Gobi matar is a familiar North Indian sabzi. Here it is portioned and plated with roti so it counts as a full lunch or dinner main in a diet plan.
Roasting or dry-sautéing cauliflower first helps it stay less soggy and more flavorful.
instructions: Heat 1/2 tbsp oil in a wide pan and saute cauliflower florets with a pinch of salt until lightly golden at the edges; set aside.
In the same pan, heat remaining oil. Add cumin, onion, then ginger-garlic; cook until onion softens.
Add tomato and dry spices with salt. Cook until the masala thickens.
Return cauliflower, add peas and a splash of water. Cover and cook until florets are tender but not mushy, 8–10 minutes.
Finish with garam masala and coriander. Serve with rotis.
stepNotes: Do not over-steam cauliflower or it collapses into the gravy.
Frozen peas need only a few minutes; add them later than fresh peas if needed.
A wide pan helps moisture escape so the sabzi stays semi-dry for roti.
Kasuri methi at the end is optional for a dhaba-style aroma.
Prep florets similar in size for even cooking.

variationSections.title: Masala styles
items: Drier sukhi sabzi: skip extra water and cook uncovered.
Gravy style: add more tomato and a little water for a wetter curry.
Add capsicum for extra vegetable volume.

variationSections.title: Plate adjustments
items: Millet roti for a gluten-free variation.
Pair with plain dal when you want more protein.
